verification_gap: A related class-action and the company's later relaunch followed; the figure cited is the PIPC administrative sanction.

verification_gap: The controller (Budapest Bank) could seek judicial review; appeal outcome not confirmed.

title: India (Mumbai): court denies anticipatory bail to maker of Amitabh Bachchan deepfakes
primary_source_id: Mumbai Sessions Court | Anticipatory bail denied; ruling July 6, 2024
paper_use_note: An Indian court refused pre-arrest protection to a man booked by cyber police for making deepfake videos misusing actor Amitabh Bachchan's likeness for product promotion.
verification_gap: Interim bail ruling at the investigation stage; no final conviction confirmed.

title: India (Delhi): police FIRs over AI-generated/deepfake election content
primary_source_id: Delhi Police | Five FIRs registered during the 2024 Lok Sabha campaign
paper_use_note: Delhi Police registered multiple FIRs over AI-generated/deepfake videos of senior politicians during the 2024 election.
verification_gap: FIR (complaint-registration) stage only; defendants/charges sustained not confirmed. | VERIFIED: the documented five-FIRs set maps to Jan 2025 Delhi Assembly polls, not the 2024 Lok Sabha campaign.

title: SEC v. Presto Automation Inc. (first AI-washing action vs a public company)
primary_source_id: SEC | Securities Act Rel. 11352 / Exchange Act Rel. 102177, Admin. Proc. 3-22413, Jan 14, 2025
ai_system_type: voice/speech-recognition AI for drive-thru order taking
paper_use_note: SEC's first AI-washing action against a public company; settled cease-and-desist finding Presto misrepresented that proprietary AI eliminated human order-taking.
verification_gap: Settled without admitting/denying findings.

title: SEC & DOJ v. Ilit Raz (Joonko Diversity AI recruiting fraud)
primary_source_id: SEC + DOJ SDNY | SEC Litigation Release LR-26020, June 11, 2024; parallel DOJ indictment
paper_use_note: SEC alleges Raz defrauded investors of ~$21M with false statements about Joonko's AI, customers and revenue; parallel DOJ securities/wire-fraud charges.
verification_gap: Litigated allegations, not adjudicated; criminal matter presumes innocence.

title: SEC v. Rimar Capital (false AI trading claims)
primary_source_id: SEC | SEC Press Release 2024-167, Oct 10, 2024 (settled order)
paper_use_note: SEC settled finding the respondents raised ~$3.7M from 45 investors on claims of AI-driven automated trading that did not exist; disgorgement, penalties, and bars imposed.
verification_gap: Settled without admitting/denying findings.

title: SEC v. QZ Asset Management (AI pre-IPO fraud)
primary_source_id: SEC | SEC Litigation Release LR-26083, Aug 27, 2024
paper_use_note: SEC alleges QZ defrauded investors of at least $6M with false AI, banking-relationship and IPO claims, then went dark.
verification_gap: Litigated allegations not yet adjudicated; offshore respondents may limit finality.

title: SEC v. Destiny Robotics (exaggerated humanoid AI robot claims)
primary_source_id: SEC | SEC charges settled Oct 15, 2024
paper_use_note: SEC charged that Destiny raised ~$141K on grossly exaggerated AI-robot capability claims; settled with disgorgement and a $50,000 penalty.
verification_gap: Anchor confirmed via legal-press summary; SEC order PDF not directly fetched.

title: SEC & DOJ v. Alexander Beckman (GameOn AI chatbot startup fraud)
primary_source_id: SEC + DOJ N.D. Cal. | SEC Litigation Release LR-26232, Jan 23, 2025; DOJ indictment 25-CR-00012
paper_use_note: SEC/DOJ allege Beckman defrauded investors of $60M+ at the AI startup GameOn via forged bank statements, a fake audit and fabricated revenue.
verification_gap: Charged conduct centers on financial forgery rather than AI falsity; allegations unadjudicated. | VERIFIED: SEC release dated 24 Jan 2025; DOJ indictment unsealed 21 Jan 2025.

title: SEC v. Nathan Fuller & Privvy Investments (fake AI crypto trading bots)
primary_source_id: SEC | SEC civil complaint filed May 28, 2026 (~$12.3M, 150+ investors)
paper_use_note: SEC alleges Fuller ran a $12.3M Ponzi promising 40-50% returns from non-existent AI trading bots, later using ChatGPT-generated KYC letters to stall withdrawals.
verification_gap: Very recent complaint (May 2026), not adjudicated; confirmed via financial-trade press.

title: Ogletree v. Cleveland State University (remote-proctoring room scan unconstitutional)
primary_source_id: U.S. District Court, N.D. Ohio | No. 1:21-cv-500, opinion Aug 22, 2022
ai_system_type: remote exam proctoring with room-scan surveillance
paper_use_note: A federal court granted summary judgment for the student, holding a public university's pre-exam room scan was an unreasonable Fourth Amendment search.
verification_gap: Holding is about the search, not an AI-grading/decision algorithm specifically.

title: ACLU charge v. Intuit & HireVue (AI video-interview disability/race discrimination)
primary_source_id: EEOC + Colorado Civil Rights Division | ACLU charge filed Mar 19, 2025
paper_use_note: ACLU alleges HireVue's AI video interview, lacking captioning, discriminated against a deaf Indigenous employee under the ADA, Title VII and Colorado law.
verification_gap: Administrative charge, not adjudicated; Intuit denies and HireVue says no AI-backed assessment was used.

title: Harper v. Sirius XM Radio (AI resume-screening race discrimination class action)
primary_source_id: U.S. District Court, E.D. Michigan | No. 2:25-cv-12403 (E.D. Mich.), filed Aug 4, 2025
paper_use_note: Plaintiff alleges Sirius XM's AI hiring tool used race-proxy data points causing disparate treatment/impact after he was rejected for ~149 of 150 applications.
verification_gap: Recently filed complaint; allegations unadjudicated and class status not certified.

title: ACLU complaint v. Aon (AI hiring assessments)
primary_source_id: FTC complaint + EEOC charge | ACLU FTC complaint and EEOC charge filed May 2024
ai_system_type: AI personality, video-interview and cognitive-ability hiring assessments
paper_use_note: ACLU alleges Aon marketed 'bias-free' AI hiring assessments that unlawfully screen out applicants with disabilities and certain racial groups.
verification_gap: Administrative complaint/charge only; not adjudicated, no agency finding confirmed. | VERIFIED: FTC complaint May 2024 but EEOC charges date to late 2023; unadjudicated.
